sutta: now the brahmin januszoni came to visit the exalted 1 and on coming to him greeted him courteously sat down at 1 side so seated the brahmin januszoni said this to the exalted 1 for my part master gautama i say this i hold this view there is no 1 subject to death who does not fear and fall trembling at the thought of death and the buddha said well brahman there is such a 1 but there is also 1 subject to death who does not fear who does not fall the trembling at the thought of death and of what sort is that 1 who being subject to death fears and falls are trembling at the thought of death in this case brahman a certain 1 is not freed from passions not freed from lust not free from desire affection from thirst and fever not free from craving then a grievous sickness afflicts such a 1 thus afflicted by grievous sickness it occurs to him alas the passions that i love will leave me or i shall leave the passions that i love thereupon he grieves and wails laments and beats the breasts and falls into utter bewilderment this 1 brahman being subject to death is afraid he falls a trembling at the thought of death again brahman here a certain 1 who as regards body is not freed from lust desire affection thirst and fever is not free from craving then a grievous sickness afflicts him thus afflicted it occurs to him alas the body that i love will leave me or i shall leave the body that i love thereupon he grieves wails and falls into utter bewilderment this 1 brahman being subject to death is afraid he falls a trembling at the thought of death yet again brahman here a certain 1 has done no good deed has done no profitable deed has given no shelter to the timid he has done evil cruel wrongful deeds then a grievous sickness afflicts such a 1 thus afflicted by grievous sickness it occurs to him alas i have done no good deed i have done no profitable deed i have given no shelter to the timid i have done evil cruel wrongful deeds to the doom of those who do such deeds hereafter i am going there upon he grieves wails and falls into utter bewilderment this 1 brahman being subject to death is afraid he falls a trembling at the thought of death yet again brahmin here a certain 1 is doubtful full of perplexity has come to no conclusion as to true dhamma he grieves and wails laments and beats the breath and falls into utter bewilderment this 1 also being subject to death is afraid he falls the trembling at the thought of death thus these 4 beings subject to death are afraid and of what sword brahmin is he who subject to death is not afraid does not fall trembling at the thought of death then the opposite the vice versa so this is about the person who is very frightened when death comes the first 1 is he is still very attached to the passions and a lot of desire a lot of loves so that he cannot bear to part from his passions the second 1 is is attached to the body and so when he the body is about to die he can't stand it and the third 1 has done no good deeds this 1 is very common a lot of people they don't have enough good deeds and they have no confidence when they're about to go rest people who have done a lot of good deeds and they are very happy at the last moment they are confident and the fourth 1 is is doubtful that person does not know dhamma so he does not know how he came to the world he doesn't know where he's going and he's afraid some people when death comes they're afraid to close their eyes because when they close their eyes they start to see visions so they are very scared so these are the 4 types of person so the opposite are those who are not not afraid now uh i have read uh natasha earlier where the buddha said that most people after they pass away will fall into the woeful plains of rebirth according to the sutras most people when we pass away most people will fall into the woeful plains and few will come back to the human realm or to go go to the heavenly realm so because most people go into the 3 woeful planes of the hell realm animal and ghost realm when death comes they can see their place of rebirth you can if you have experience looking at people who are about to pass away and they can tell at the last moment where they are going to be reborn so they can be some of them will scream at the last moments you know some of them sometimes they behave like an animal and go on all fours and then refuse to wear any clothes they'll just tear off their clothes so these are the the people will be very afraid when death comes which means most people so there's also a suita where the buddha said now where we should be prepared because that the deva messengers have come to tell us to give us warning who are the deva messengers when you see a person who is grievously sick you should remember that is a deva messenger to us that 1 day will also be grievously sick the second deva messenger is you see somebody is old when you see somebody's old and very near death with all hair white or no hair sparse hair and walking shaking hunchback and all that that is the sign to us now that 1 day will also be like that and then the third 1 is uh i did cops when we see a corpse that is a messenger to tell us that 1 day our body also will be a corpse and it won't be very long just a few more years time because our average lifespan is only 70 years so these are the diva messengers that we should and we should receive be heat full of the message that they are passing to us the buddha said our body is like a cancer all of us have cancer sometimes we forget you know sometimes somebody somebody with cancer they can be very alarm they they think that they are the only 1 who's got cancer i have somebody has who got cancer i've gone to see that person and he tells me i've got cancer like you're not expecting me whether what or what should he do but he forgets that all of us have cancer in the sense that we don't know when we are going to die say for example a person a person has cancer he thinks he's got maybe another a few months to live but then even for the rest of us we have a few years to live there's not very much more we all have to face the prospect of death so when we listen to the suitas we understand that's the way the world is that's the way the way things are so we accept it we accept death and then when death comes we are no more afraid so the buddha said we should always reflect on that the reflection of death we should always practice now the nexus is
